summary: A solid soundboard recording of the Sep‑20‑88 MSG show with crisp audio and standout performances of Peggy‑O, Looks Like Rain and Terrapin Station.
review: The sbd capture is generally very clear – reviewers rave about the “very good” mix and audible bass, though a few note a rough start to Disc 2. Musically the concert shines on tracks like Peggy‑O, Looks Like Rain, Terrapin Station, Althea and Bertha>Cumberland, while Louie‑Louie and Mama Tried draw mixed reactions. Overall the show is regarded as one of the better 1988 dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (9/10) give 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and sound quality; only a handful note minor flaws, leading to a strong positive composite rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix with clear bass; occasional early‑Disc 2 hiccup and one reviewer mentions a slight drop‑out, but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens with a quirky Star‑Spangled‑Banner tease and a long tuning, then settles into tight mid‑tempo numbers that keep the crowd moving. The second set is highlighted by an unusual Bertha>Cumberland segue and a powerful Terrapin Station, though some feel the later part (Louie Louie, GDTRFB) loses focus.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocal phrasing on Althea is called “delightful”; guitar work described as solid, not spectacular but definitely not bad.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is praised for being “really good” and easily heard; several reviewers note a hot, sweaty lay‑down throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) gets credit for adding brass/woodwind texture during the second set and generally holding his own.
band_member_comments.Drums: No major complaints about Bill’s drumming; one reviewer mentions “post‑drums problems” but another says they noticed none.
